news.text: Thai tanker safely transits Strait of Hormuz after talks with Iran - Reuters
UK inflation holds at 3% in February ahead of likely Iran war jump - Reuters
Western powers were unable to secure shipping in the Red Sea. Hormuz will be harder - Reuters
Morning Bid: Deal, or no deal? - Reuters
Iran military spokesperson says US is negotiating with itself - Reuters
China's coal chemicals sector cashes in as Iran war crushes petrochemical competitors - Reuters

brief:
◆ Market Overview

Pre-market points to a risk-off open with SPY down 0.34% and QQQ leading declines at -0.68%. VIX spike to 35.01 (+1.98%) signals elevated fear as Iran tensions dominate headlines. Small caps bucking the trend with IWM up 0.54%.

◆ What to Watch

  • Iran Military Escalation - Rejection of Trump negotiation talks and continued airstrikes with Israel driving geopolitical premium
  • Strait of Hormuz - Thai tanker transit successful but shipping disruption fears persist after Red Sea failures
  • Energy Complex - XLE leading at +2.03% as oil supply concerns mount
  • Earnings Light - Only notable name is EQ (AMC) with -0.0618 EPS estimate
⚠ Bear Case
Hormuz closure would be catastrophic - 20% of global oil flows through the strait. Tech selling accelerates if geopolitical premium shifts capital away from growth.
⚡ Key Driver
Iran military spokesperson outright rejecting negotiations suggests this isn't a typical Middle East flare-up. Market pricing in extended conflict scenario.

◆ Sector Rotation

Clear flight to defensive commodities with Energy (+2.03%) and Materials (+1.89%) leading while growth gets hammered. Tech (-0.58%) and Comm Services (-1.40%) bleeding as duration risk meets geopolitical uncertainty. Industrials (+0.58%) benefiting from potential defense spending surge.
✦ Bull Case
Small cap outperformance (IWM +0.54%) suggests domestic focus could shield from international chaos. Energy sector setting up for multi-week run if tensions persist.

◆ Levels That Matter

  • SPY 650 - Critical support from February lows, break targets 640
  • QQQ 580 - Tech's make-or-break level, held during last VIX spike
  • VIX 40 - Fear gauge approaching panic levels, watch for reversal signals
  • XLE 95 - Energy testing multi-month highs, breakout targets 100+

Not a prediction. A preparation. Make your plan before the bell.
